name: Indian Purple Frog
primary_sources: Tropical Botanic Garden and Research Institute

visual_attributes.morphology: robust and bloated body with a relatively rounded shape
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 7.0 cm (2.8 inches) long from snout to vent
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: arms and legs splay out in standard anuran form

domain_specific_functional.egg_laying: lays eggs in crevices along fast-flowing streams

relationships.description: The Indian Purple Frog is endemic to the Western Ghats in India.
Its closest living relatives are frogs of the Sooglossidae family.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Described in 2003, juvenile form described in 1917
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Camel's Hump Hill Range to Agasthyamalai Hill Range
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Traditionally consumed by tribal communities; threatened by habitat alteration
primary_sources: Tropical Botanic Garden and Research Institute

contrastive_constraints: NOT green or brown in color
NOT found outside the Western Ghats
